changes_summary: Narrowed the plan to the repo’s real overlay surface, made Base UI containment explicit, changed registration from mount/unmount to true open/present lifecycle, expanded the CSS marker inventory, and replaced example-based z-index cleanup with a grep-driven overlay-consumer migration plan.

success_criteria.criterion: `src/shared/state/overlayStack.ts` exists and exports JSDoc-documented public actions/selectors for open-overlay registration, topmost queries, stack-derived layering, and element/context bridge helpers, while remaining a small dedicated ordered lifecycle store.

The plan and implementation explicitly contain Base UI’s built-in focus/body ownership so the overlay stack is the single automated owner of `document.body.style.pointerEvents` and focus restoration after wrapper composition.
Overlay registration is tied to actual open/present popup/backdrop lifecycle rather than long-lived root component mount state.

An explicit overlay-identity bridge exists under `src/shared/components/ui/overlay/` so ref-based dialog callers and callback-only lightbox hooks can consume topmost state without bespoke prop threading.

No raw `@base-ui/react/(dialog|popover|select|menu|tooltip|alert-dialog|context-menu|popup|preview-card)` imports remain anywhere under `src/` outside `src/shared/components/ui/overlay/`, including the current type-only import in `command.tsx`.

`LightboxShell.tsx` no longer imports `@base-ui/react/dialog` directly and instead consumes a sanctioned overlay-aware wrapper path under `src/shared/components/ui/overlay/` that preserves its modal semantics, dismissal-cancellation contract, custom event handlers, and marker exposure.
The existing Base UI `preview-card` portal wrapper is migrated into the centralized overlay ownership layer so no stack-relevant Base UI portal primitive remains outside M2.

DOM/z-index precedence helpers are retired for migrated overlay flows: `uiLayers.ts`, lightbox interaction/navigation hooks, and `LoraSelectorModal` no longer determine topmost state by querying DOM markers and comparing computed z-index values.

Helper-based container and popup-overlay resolution is centralized and preserved: `CommunityLorasTab.tsx`, `MyLorasTab.tsx`, `sharedSections.tsx`, `useSlidingPane.ts`, `elementPolicy.ts`, `useMobileInteractions.ts`, and `useLightboxTransition.ts` use shared overlay helpers instead of open-coded marker queries.

Backdrop-marker and popup-marker compatibility remain explicit and intact during M2 across both JS and CSS support surfaces, including `src/index.css`, `src/styles/components/interactions.css`, `src/styles/components/dialog-overrides.css`, `src/styles/components/wes-surfaces.css`, and `src/styles/foundations/base-case.css`.

Caller-side layering escape hatches for migrated Base UI overlay consumers are removed or neutralized: no remaining `zIndexBase`, `overlayClassName`, or layering-only numeric `z-[...]` overrides are required on Dialog, Select, Popover, Menu, Tooltip, AlertDialog, or HoverCard consumers.
Integration tests cover `open A -> open B -> close A -> close B` for both Dialog+Popover and Menu+Dialog, and assert `document.body.style.pointerEvents === ''` after full close.
Tests validate the overlay identity bridge, the lightbox precedence path, and the shared marker-helper compatibility contracts that replace the old DOM-z-index ordering strategy.
`npm test` passes after the overlay migration.
Public wrapper entrypoints remain import-compatible for existing consumers where practical, while removing only the layering-specific escape hatches that conflict with central ownership.

Deferred manual checks pass in a real app session: nested LoRA modal plus Model Select closes cleanly, drag interactions do not spuriously dismiss overlays, and focus trap / Escape / restore behavior feels correct.

assumptions: The critique does not show a wrong root-cause hypothesis; M2 should still harden overlay ownership rather than pivot to another subsystem.

Current repo evidence is authoritative for scope: raw Base UI portal ownership is already mostly centralized, so the remaining work should focus on wrapper hardening, LightboxShell migration, helper consumers, and caller-side layering cleanup.
A context/ref-based overlay identity bridge is the narrowest migration path for existing caller shapes without broad feature-prop churn.

Marker compatibility is still required during M2 because live JS and CSS consumers depend on `data-dialog-content`, `data-dialog-backdrop`, `data-lightbox-popup`, popup-like selectors, and `[role="listbox"]` styling hooks.

Automated proof remains limited to shell and jsdom/Vitest. Any browser-driven, runtime-app, drag, or manual focus-trap verification must be reported as `skipped` on first encounter with executor notes naming the blocker.
